info.name: Pawffy API — Unified
info.description:
Complete Pawffy backend API collection — `{{baseUrl}}`

## What this collection includes
- Customer / core backend APIs
- Admin-protected endpoints
- Vendor onboarding, live vendor app, account/security, and adoption flows
- Public discovery, support, and static-content endpoints

## Recommended usage
1. Use `baseUrl` for your current environment. Default is local: `{{localBaseUrl}}`.
2. Start with `🔐 Auth` for customer/general flows or `Vendor Auth` for partner flows.
3. Use the saved variables (`token`, `adminToken`, IDs, OTP/token helpers) as requests populate them.
4. For production testing, switch `baseUrl` to `{{productionBaseUrl}}` and follow the production notes in the vendor account/security requests.

## Folder layout
- Core app folders come from the general backend collection.
- Vendor-specific folders are appended after the core folders so all APIs live in one importable file.

## Security and behavior notes
- Protected routes require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` unless they specifically use `adminToken`.
- JWT sessions use `tokenVersion`, so logout/password change/contact verification can invalidate older tokens.
- Wallet payment routes only work when `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true`.
- In non-production, some vendor verification flows may return OTP/token helpers for local testing; production does not return those secrets.

## Important variables
baseUrl · localBaseUrl · productionBaseUrl · token · adminToken · userId · petId · serviceId · bookingId · businessId · requestId · stripeAccountId · connectOnboardingUrl · supabaseAccessToken


## Stripe Connect (vendor payouts)
- Folder: **💸 Vendor Payouts (Stripe Connect)**
- Endpoints: `GET /api/vendor/payouts/check`, `POST /api/vendor/payouts/onboard`, `GET /api/vendor/payouts/status`
- Production: `https://pawffy-backend-yyed.onrender.com` (set `baseUrl` = `{{productionBaseUrl}}`)
- Local: `http://localhost:5001`
- Variables: stripeAccountId · connectOnboardingUrl


## Reviews
Folder **⭐ Reviews (Customer ↔ Vendor)**: customer rates vendor + vendor rates customer after completed booking. Set `vendorId` (business UUID) and `bookingId`. Switch `token` between customer and partner JWTs as noted in each step.

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Test flow (run in order):
1. Set collection vars `supabaseAnonKey` (publishable key) and `loginPhoneNumber`
2. **STEP 1** — Supabase Send Phone OTP
3. **STEP 2** — Supabase Verify Phone OTP (saves `supabaseAccessToken`)
4. **STEP 3** — Session (Customer) → saves Pawffy `token`

Vendor signup: run steps 1–2, then **Supabase Vendor Register**.

request.description:
Calls Supabase directly (not Pawffy). Sends SMS OTP to `loginPhoneNumber`.

Requires `supabaseAnonKey` = your **Publishable key** from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API Keys.

exec: console.log('No access_token in response. Paste SMS code into supabaseOtp and retry.');

request.description:
Paste the 6-digit SMS code into collection variable `supabaseOtp`, then send.

On success, saves `supabaseAccessToken` automatically.

exec: console.log('Token & userId saved from Supabase session');

request.description:
Exchange `supabaseAccessToken` (from STEP 2) for a Pawffy API JWT.

If you see **accessToken is not allowed to be empty**, run STEP 1 and STEP 2 first.

exec: console.log('Vendor token saved from Supabase register');

request.description: Create a vendor account after Supabase phone OTP. Phone comes from the verified Supabase session.

exec: console.log('Token cleared. All sessions on all devices are now invalid.');

request.description:
Increments tokenVersion in DB → every JWT issued before this call is instantly rejected.
All devices are logged out simultaneously.

item.name: 📅 Bookings — Full Flow
item.description:
Complete booking flow:
1. GET /vets/:vetId/services  → select service + price
2. GET /vets/:vetId/slots?date → pick time slot
3. GET /pets                    → pick pet
4. Client collects symptoms/reason
5. POST /bookings               → create booking
6. GET /payments/summary/:id   → price breakdown
7. POST /payments/apply-coupon → optional coupon
8a. POST /payments/create-intent → card/net_banking (Stripe)
8b. POST /payments/confirm       → wallet (disabled unless WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true)
8c. POST /payments/verify        → sync Stripe after payment sheet
9. GET /bookings/:id            → confirmation screen

Status: customers can PATCH cancelled only; admins can set any status.

body.raw:
{
  "petId": "{{petId}}",
  "vetId": "{{vetId}}",
  "serviceId": "{{serviceId}}",
  "bookingType": "veterinarian",
  "bookingDate": "2026-07-15",
  "bookingTime": "11:00",
  "reasonForVisit": "My dog has been lethargic for 2 days",
  "symptoms": "Vomiting,Lethargy,Loss of appetite",
  "symptomsDuration": "2 Days ago",
  "notes": "Please check ears as well"
}

request.description:
bookingType: veterinarian | grooming | walking | training | boarding | sitting | sitter | poop_scooper | transport

symptomsDuration: Today | 2 Days ago | A week ago | More than a week

Conflict check: returns 409 if that slot is already booked.

Returns: servicePrice + platformFee($5) + tax(5%) - discount = total
Also returns pawPoints earned.

item.name: STEP 8a — Create Stripe PaymentIntent (Card / Net Banking)

request.description:
paymentMethod: card | net_banking

Returns clientSecret → pass to flutter_stripe:
  Stripe.instance.initPaymentSheet(paymentIntentClientSecret: clientSecret)
  Stripe.instance.presentPaymentSheet()

Booking confirmed automatically via Stripe webhook.

Wallet payment — deducts from `/api/wallet` balance.

Requires `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true`. Top up first via **💰 Wallet → Top Up Wallet**.
Returns 402 if balance is insufficient.

Call after flutter_stripe payment sheet closes.
Syncs Stripe status with DB if webhook hasn't fired yet.
Returns: stripeStatus, paymentStatus, bookingStatus, appointmentId
Returns: appointmentId, vet, pet, dateTimeFormatted, totalPaid, paymentStatus
🔒 Requires role: admin

Customers (booking owner) may only set status to "cancelled".
Admins may set: pending | confirmed | completed | cancelled.
Confirmation/completion for customers happens via payment flow or admin action.

🔒 Requires role: admin

Admins may set: pending | confirmed | completed | cancelled.
Customers can only cancel via "Update Booking Status".

item.name: ⭐ Reviews (Customer ↔ Vendor)
item.description:
Two-way reviews after a **completed** booking.

**Variables needed:** `token` (switch customer/partner JWT), `bookingId`, `vendorId` (same as businessId).

### Run order
1. Complete a booking end-to-end (pay + vendor complete)
2. Set `token` = **customer** JWT → **STEP 1** Review Vendor
3. **STEP 2** Public list (no auth needed)
4. Set `token` = **partner** JWT → **STEP 3** Vendor inbox + **STEP 4** Reply
5. Still partner JWT → **STEP 5** Review Customer + **STEP 6** List written
6. Set `token` = **customer** JWT → **STEP 7** Reviews about me

Rules: booking `completed`; one review per booking per direction; rating 1–5.
Production: `https://pawffy-backend-yyed.onrender.com`

request.description: Use **customer** JWT in `token`. Body needs completed `bookingId`. Also sets `businessId` if you use that var — set `vendorId` = business UUID.
Public. No auth required.
Use **partner** JWT.
Use **partner** JWT. `businessReviewId` auto-saved from STEP 1.
Use **partner** JWT. Same completed `bookingId`.

item.name: STEP 6 — Vendor List Customer Reviews I Wrote

request.description: Use **partner** JWT.
Use **customer** JWT.

Returns: otherUser, lastMessage, updatedAt, unreadCount (orange dot)
Optional: ?search=name filters by other user's name

Tap a vet profile → call this to get or create a conversation.
Returns conversationId + otherUser info.
Set {{receiverId}} to the other user's ID. Creates conversation if first message.
Call when user opens a conversation — clears the unread (orange dot) count.

item.name: 💳 Payments
item.description:
Stripe payment gateway for bookings. Rate limited: 20 req / 15 min.

Mobile flow:
1. `GET /api/payments/config` → publishableKey
2. `POST /create-intent` → clientSecret
3. flutter_stripe Payment Sheet
4. `POST /verify` → sync status

Webhook: `POST /api/payments/webhook` (Stripe Dashboard)

request.description: Returns Stripe publishable key + currency for flutter_stripe initialization.
Returns: serviceName, servicePrice, platformFee($5), tax(5%), discount, total, pawPoints

paymentMethod: card | net_banking
Returns clientSecret for flutter_stripe

⚠️ Disabled by default (503) unless server env WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true.
Use Stripe create-intent + verify for production card/net banking payments.
🔒 Must own the booking linked to the payment intent.

item.name: 💰 Wallet
item.description: Wallet balance and transactions. Requires `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true` on the server for top-up, withdraw, and booking wallet payments.

request.description: Dev-only instant credit. For production use **Stripe Top-Up Intent** instead.
Creates Stripe PaymentIntent for wallet top-up. Use `clientSecret` in flutter_stripe, then call **Verify Stripe Top-Up**.
Deducts balance (Send to Bank flow). Requires sufficient balance.

request.description: Send Bearer token for user profile + notifications. Without token: public data only (categories, partners, banner, quote). Do NOT send userId in body.
🔒 Requires auth. Returns private profile only for self or admin.
🌐 Public. Returns partners within 25km (no email/phone exposed).

item.name: 🚨 Lost Pets
item.description:
Owner flow — report **your own** missing pet.

Required: pet photo(s), name, color, breed, gender, description, last-seen location.
Optional: age, height, weight.

Upload images as files (`images` field, max 3). JSON body with image URLs is still supported for testing.

formdata.description: Pet photo (JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 2MB). Add up to 3 files with the same field name.

formdata.value: Last seen near park wearing red collar

request.description: Owner reports their missing pet. Upload 1–3 photos using field name `images`. Age/height/weight are optional.

item.name: 🔍 Found Pets
item.description:
Stranger flow — report a pet you **saw/found** but do not own.

Required: photo(s), color, description, location.
Optional: breed, gender (defaults to `Unknown` if omitted).

You do NOT need to know the pet's name, exact breed, age, height, or weight.

formdata.description: Photo of the pet you saw (JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 2MB)

formdata.value: Medium-sized friendly dog near the station. Looked lost, no collar visible.

request.description: For people who spotted a stray/lost pet. Breed and gender are optional.
Returns lostPets and foundPets arrays combined.

item.name: 🚶 Walking Bookings
item.description:
partnerId must be a user with role=partner.
Pets in selectedPetList must belong to the authenticated user.
paymentStatus is server-controlled.

request.description:
partnerId must be a user with role=partner.
selectedPetList must contain pet IDs owned by the authenticated user.
paymentStatus is set server-side (always Pending) — do not send it in the body.

item.name: Vendor Auth
item.description: Mobile auth: Supabase phone OTP in the app, then `POST /api/auth/session` with `accessToken` to get a Pawffy JWT. Vendor signup: `POST /api/auth/vendor/register`.

item.name: 💸 Vendor Payouts (Stripe Connect)
item.description:
Vendor payout onboarding via Stripe Connect Express.

**Production baseUrl:** `https://pawffy-backend-yyed.onrender.com`

Run order (partner JWT in `token`):
1. **Check Onboarded** — fast DB check (`onboarded` boolean)
2. **Onboard** — returns Stripe `url` + `stripeAccountId`. Open URL, finish Express onboarding (test: SSN `000-00-0000`, routing `110000000`, account `000123456789`)
3. **Get Payout Status** — live Stripe sync; expect `payoutsEnabled: true`, `onboarded: true`

Card payments are blocked until `payoutsEnabled` is true. Wallet payments credit the vendor wallet and do not need Connect.

Flutter: open onboard `url` in WebView; intercept `/vendor/payouts/return` and `/vendor/payouts/refresh`, then call Check/Status.

exec: console.log('onboarded:', r.data?.onboarded, '| payoutsEnabled:', r.data?.payoutsEnabled, '| hasStripeAccount:', r.data?.hasStripeAccount);

request.description: Fast DB-only check. Use to gate Flutter UI (show "Set up payouts" vs "Payouts active").

exec: console.log('Open this URL to finish Stripe onboarding:', r.data.url);

request.description: Creates/retrieves Stripe Express account and returns onboarding URL.

exec: console.log('payoutsEnabled:', r.data?.payoutsEnabled, '| onboarded:', r.data?.onboarded, '| requirementsDue:', r.data?.requirementsDue);

request.description: Live Stripe sync (retrieves account + updates DB). Call after finishing onboarding.

item.name: 2. Onboarding (before approval)
item.description:
Use only while `verificationStatus` is `incomplete` or `rejected`.
Blocked once status is `pending` or `verified`.

item.name: 8.5 Account & Security
item.description:
Phase 2 vendor account-security flows.

Non-production: the API may return `phoneOtp` / `emailOtp` and the collection scripts will save them automatically.

Production: the API will not return those secrets. Use the OTP received over SMS and the email OTP delivered to the new email address, then paste the real value into the corresponding collection variable before running the verify request.

request.description: Starts a secure phone update. In production, expect the OTP to be delivered outside the API response.
Completes phone verification using the real OTP. In production, paste the SMS OTP into `phoneOtp` first.
Starts a secure email change. In non-production the API may return `otp`. In production the OTP is delivered by Twilio SendGrid (or SMTP fallback) and is not returned.
Completes the email swap using the 6-digit OTP. In production, paste the OTP from the email into `emailOtp` first.

item.name: 8.6 Adoptions
item.description: Vendor adoption workflow for pet-based applications. Use the wallet payment request only when wallet payments are enabled in the backend; otherwise use the Stripe intent + verify sequence.

request.description: Upload one document at a time using field name `document`. Supported types mirror other vendor document uploads.
Use only when `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true` in the backend environment.
Run after Stripe confirms the intent succeeded; this finalizes the adoption status in the backend.

formdata.value: The app crashes when I try to start a walk booking.

request.description: Contact support with optional file attachment. Categories: technical_issue, account_issue, booking_issue, payment_issue, general.
